headline: Hungary celebrates 'return to Europe' after Orban's crushing electoral defeat
description: Péter Magyar's party, Tisza, won more than 53% of the vote in the legislative elections, far ahead of the prime minister's Fidesz, which managed barely 38%. Tisza is expected to secure 138 out of the 199 seats in Parliament, giving it a constitutional supermajority.

headline: Trump's threat to blockade Strait of Hormuz leaves more questions than answers
'Enough of war!': Pope Leo XIV publicly opposes Trump, who fiercely criticizes him in return
'They are asked to be both Madonna and whore': Photographer Philippa James captures her teenage daughter's daily life on social media
description: In her series 'Once a Slag…,' the British photographer explores the contradictions facing young people as they navigate identity, social pressures and the constant exposure of their lives online.

headline: 'It is the working-class youth that pays for the pensions of wealthy, elderly Parisians'
description: The mayors of Paris and Saint-Denis would do well to take inspiration from a new book by historians Gilles Postel-Vinay and Jean-Laurent Rosenthal, which sheds light on private wealth accumulation in the French capital, says Pierre-Cyrille Hautcœur.

headline: Lebanese mourn victims of 'Black Wednesday': 'We are not just numbers'
description: On April 9 in Qamatiyé, near Beirut, the family of Fatima Amhaz buried the 33-year-old pharmacist who was killed by an Israeli airstrike the previous day. That same day, at least 357 people lost their lives in Lebanon, marking the deadliest day recorded since the war between Israel and Hezbollah began on March 2.

headline: 'Far from modernizing Hungary, Orban's economic policy has led the country into a dead end'
description: The ousted prime minister has shown that economic populism, steeped in nationalism and corroded by corruption, ultimately results in stagnation. Hungarians ended up learning the lesson, writes Le Monde columnist Stéphane Lauer.

headline: French lawmakers to debate bill to return looted colonial-era art
US military says to begin naval blockade of Strait of Hormuz after failed talks over Iran war
Migrant workers in India leave as gas runs out: 'I don't want to starve to death'

French government pushes for conversion of empty office spaces into 8,200 homes in Paris region
Tearful Van Aert finally wins Paris-Roubaix cycling Monument
World Press Photo winner Abdulmonam Eassa on his reporting in Sudan: 'This is a story that resonates very personally'
In Algeria, the pope follows in the footsteps of the country's Christian history and reaches out to Muslims
description: On Monday, Leo XIV will become the first pontiff in history to visit Algeria, birthplace of the renowned Christian philosopher Saint Augustine. It was in Hippo, now Annaba, that the theologian wrote his most significant works in the fourth century.

headline: The planning battle to revive Tour Montparnasse, Paris's only skyscraper
description: After more than 10 years of negotiations between Paris City Hall and the tower's co-owners, the transformation of the now-empty Tour Montparnasse and its property complex is set to begin in autumn 2026.
byline: Audrey Lagadec,  Emeline Cazi and  Isabelle Regnier

headline: 'Do you have any regrets about having to kill?' On Trans-Siberian Railway, Russian soldiers recount time in Ukraine
description: 'On Board the Trans-Siberian' (2/2). As the train speeds toward the Russian Far East, between Novosibirsk and Vladivostok, the brutality of the war in Ukraine is at the center of conversations between soldiers on leave and 'ordinary' passengers.

headline: In Hungary, a welcome defeat for illiberalism
